description: The basic act of perceiving sound through the ears. Unlike deeper forms of listening, this represents the fundamental sensory experience of auditory perception without necessarily implying focused attention or understanding.
rationale: The word 'thao' uses the soft fricative 'th' to evoke the gentle arrival of sound waves, paired with the open vowel 'a'. The final 'o' creates a rounded, receptive quality - like the ear canal receiving sound. This FV.V structure gives the word a flowing quality while the 'a-o' sequence suggests movement from openness to focused reception.
